name: Protected Landscape Corredor Verde del Río Guadiamar
name_original: Paisaje Protegido Corredor Verde del Río Guadiamar
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Sanlúcar La Mayor traverse the Aljarafe region of Seville, characterized by its position on the edge of a ravine and fertile fields. The area features the Guadiamar Green Corridor, a protected natural space following the Guadiamar River, offering diverse Mediterranean landscapes. Olive groves, cereals, and vineyards contribute to the scenic routes, alongside areas like the Área Recreativa de las Doblas with its Mediterranean forest. The topography includes flat back roads and varied terrains suitable for cycling. Best touring cycling routes around Sanlúcar La Mayor The most popular touring cycling route is Protected Landscape Corredor Verde del Río Guadiamar, a 45.4 miles (73.0 km) trail that takes 6 hours 47 minutes to complete, offering varied landscapes along the Guadiamar River. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Cafeteria Camping Dehesa Nueva – Quema Ford loop from Sanlúcar la Mayor, a difficult 32.5 miles (52.2 km) path. This route features a mix of open fields and natural areas with moderate elevation changes. Local touring cyclists also love the Guadiamar River – Guardiamar Green Corridor loop from Sanlúcar la Mayor, a 27.3 miles (43.9 km) trail leading through the Guadiamar Green Corridor, often completed in about 3 hours 52 minutes. Touring cycling around Sanlúcar La Mayor is defined by the Guadiamar Green Corridor, fertile olive groves, and flat back roads, offering a network of routes for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Sanlúcar La Mayor are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 70 reviews. More than 1000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Sanlúcar La Mayor's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Sanlúcar La Mayor?
A: There are over 15 touring cycling routes around Sanlúcar La Mayor, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes more than 1000 times.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in Sanlúcar La Mayor?
A: The routes cater to various abilities, with 1 easy route, 11 moderate routes, and 4 difficult routes. This ensures there's something for everyone, from casual rides to more challenging excursions.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see on touring cycling routes around Sanlúcar La Mayor?
A: Touring cycling routes in Sanlúcar La Mayor traverse diverse landscapes, including the protected &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988535297/protected-landscape-corredor-verde-del-rio-guadiamar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiamar Green Corridor&lt;/a&gt; with its Mediterranean flora. You'll also cycle through fertile fields of olive groves, cereals, and vineyards, and experience the unique topography of the Aljarafe region, characterized by ravines and flat back roads.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Sanlúcar La Mayor?
A: Yes, several routes offer circular options, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17244458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiamar River – Guardiamar Green Corridor loop from Sanlúcar la Mayor&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice, taking you through the scenic Guadiamar Green Corridor.

Q: What landmarks or points of interest can I explore along touring cycling routes?
A: Along the routes, you can discover various points of interest. Notable landmarks include the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1606627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge Over the Pudio River&lt;/a&gt;, the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7086624" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of Loreto&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3113504" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiamar Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offering panoramic views of the natural surroundings.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or rest along the routes?
A: Yes, some routes offer convenient stops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38223048" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cafeteria Camping Dehesa Nueva – Quema Ford loop from Sanlúcar la Mayor&lt;/a&gt; passes by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2858510" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cafeteria Camping Dehesa Nueva&lt;/a&gt;, providing an opportunity for a break and refreshments.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Sanlúcar La Mayor?
A: Sanlúcar La Mayor benefits from an abundance of nature and sunshine, making it suitable for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for touring, avoiding the peak heat of summer while still enjoying good weather.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in the area?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations are not always explicit, the Guadiamar Green Corridor, with its rehabilitated paths and diverse landscapes, offers sections that are generally suitable for families. Look for routes categorized as 'easy' or 'moderate' for a more relaxed experience.

Q: Can I bring my dog on touring cycling routes in Sanlúcar La Mayor?
A: Many natural areas and paths in Spain are dog-friendly, especially if your dog is well-behaved and kept on a leash. However, it's always advisable to check local regulations for specific routes within the Guadiamar Green Corridor or other protected areas to ensure compliance.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Sanlúcar La Mayor?
A: The touring cycling routes around Sanlúcar La Mayor are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 70 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the beauty of the Guadiamar Green Corridor, and the peacefulness of the olive groves and vineyards.

Q: What is the typical length of touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Route lengths vary significantly, from shorter rides to longer excursions. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40332369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiamar Footbridge – Dehesa of the Guadiamar loop from Sanlúcar la Mayor&lt;/a&gt; is around 23.7 miles (38.1 km), while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988535297/protected-landscape-corredor-verde-del-rio-guadiamar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Protected Landscape Corredor Verde del Río Guadiamar&lt;/a&gt; extends to 45.4 miles (73.0 km).

Q: Are there any routes that connect Sanlúcar La Mayor to Seville?
A: Yes, some routes extend towards the provincial capital. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32047824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tower of Gold – Seville City Hall loop from Sanlúcar la Mayor&lt;/a&gt; is an example, offering a longer ride that takes you towards iconic landmarks in Seville.
tips: It has tables and benches where you can rest enjoying the abundant vegetation of the Guadiamar river. Right next door is a botanical trail and visitor center.

The marked Guadiamar trail is more than 60 km long, it is an ecological corridor that joins Sierra Morena and Doñana. But this site also reminds us of one of the biggest environmental disasters in Europe - when in 1998 the Aznalcóllar mining dam burst and caused a polluting spill in this area.
Famous pass through which, once a year, pilgrims pass on their way to El Rocío. If we find too much water, we can always use the bridge a few meters upstream.

The Mirador de la Cárcava also called Balcón del Guadiamar is an old landfill that has been transformed into a viewpoint. It has views of the Guadiamar river, olive groves and the rest of the region: municipalities of Pilas, Torre Guadiamar, Caño de la Teja, Huévar del Aljarafe etc.
